summary: Create the dashboard/src/dashboard/templates/partials/costs/ directory; Write failing tests for the window parameter parsing helper (_WINDOW_DAYS mapping and _parse_window function). Tests: valid window strings map to correct days (24h→1, 7d→7, 30d→30, all→3650), invalid/missing defaults to 7, returns int.; Implement _WINDOW_DAYS dict and _parse_window(request) helper in app.py. Maps window query param strings to days integers. Default 7d. Add import of costs data functions.; Write failing tests for the costs page template (GET /costs). Tests: returns 200, contains HTMX polling sections for all 7 partials (summary, by-project, by-account, by-role, trend, events, runs), contains window selector buttons (24h/7d/30d/all), 7d button is active by default, sections use morph:innerHTML swap, nav bar 'costs' link is active.; Replace costs.html stub with full page template. Window selector with 4 buttons using Alpine.js state + htmx custom event 'costs-window-changed'. 7 inline polling sections (not using macro, since hx-vals needed for dynamic window param). Each section has data-cost-section, hx-get, hx-trigger with poll interval (summary 30s, charts 60s, events 30s, runs 60s), hx-vals='js:{window: currentWindow}', hx-swap='morph:innerHTML', skeleton placeholders. Update GET /costs route to pass window param to template context.; Write failing tests for GET /costs/partials/summary route. Tests: returns 200 with mocked get_cost_summary data, renders 4 metric cards (total spend, avg cost/task, active accounts, cap events), handles empty data gracefully, respects window query param.; Add GET /costs/partials/summary route in app.py. Calls get_cost_summary(db, days=days). Create summary.html partial template with 4 metric cards in a grid, aggregating across projects: total spend ($X.XX), avg cost/task ($X.XX), active accounts (N), cap events (N). Use bg-gray-800 cards with colored accent borders.; Write failing tests for GET /costs/partials/by-project route. Tests: returns 200 with mocked get_cost_by_project data, renders Chart.js horizontal stacked bar config in JS, uses consistent color palette, handles empty data.; Add GET /costs/partials/by-project route in app.py. Create by_project.html partial with horizontal stacked bar chart (Chart.js). X-axis: cost in USD, Y-axis: project names. Segments by model. Uses getOrDestroyChart pattern and htmx:afterSettle init. 10-color palette from memory_graphs.html.; Write failing tests for GET /costs/partials/by-account route. Tests: returns 200, renders doughnut chart JS config, renders account table with spend/invocations/cap_events/last_cap/status columns, status badge colors (capped=red, active=green), handles empty data.; Add GET /costs/partials/by-account route in app.py. Create by_account.html partial with two-column layout: left doughnut chart (spend by account) with datalabels percentages, right table with columns account_name, spend, invocations, cap_events, last_cap (timeago filter), status (color-coded badge). Uses group_top_n for chart data.

Completed all remaining 12 steps. Created 4 new partial templates: by_role.html (horizontal stacked bar per project/role/model), trend.html (daily line chart one dataset per project), events.html (chronological feed with red/green event type borders), runs.html (two-level Alpine.js expandable drilldown table with invocation detail). Wrote tests for each partial (steps 11/13/15/17), verified all pass after template creation (steps 12/14/16/18). Added window selector integration tests and partial failure resilience tests (steps 19-22) — all passed immediately because routes, try/except, and window wiring were already implemented in earlier steps. Final count: 100 tests in test_costs_page.py, 192 total tests pass with 0 failures.
